```r
# Load required packages
library(haven)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(labelled)
library(readr)

# Define file paths
wave8_file <- "data/input/ns8_2015_derived.tab"
wave9_file <- "data/input/ns9_2022_derived_variables.tab"

# Load datasets
wave8_data <- read_delim(wave8_file, delim = "\t", show_col_types = FALSE)
wave9_data <- read_delim(wave9_file, delim = "\t", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Merge datasets using full_join by NSID
merged_data <- full_join(wave8_data, wave9_data, by = "NSID")

# Define income value labels for both waves
income_labels <- c(
  "1" = "less than 25",
  "2" = "25 to 50",
  "3" = "50 to 90",
  "4" = "90 to 140",
  "5" = "140 to 240",
  "6" = "240 to 300",
  "7" = "300 to 350",
  "8" = "350 to 400",
  "9" = "400 to 500",
  "10" = "500 to 600",
  "11" = "600 to 700",
  "12" = "700 to 800",
  "13" = "800 to 900",
  "14" = "900 to 1200",
  "15" = "1200 to 1400",
  "16" = "more than 1400"
)

# Standard missing value labels
missing_labels <- c(
  "-9" = "Refusal",
  "-8" = "Don't know/insufficient information",
  "-1" = "Item not applicable",
  "-3" = "Not asked/interviewed",
  "-2" = "Script error/lost"
)

# Function to recode and label income variable
recode_income <- function(x, var_name) {
  # Replace NA with -3 (not asked/interviewed)
  x[is.na(x)] <- -3
  
  # Create numeric vector
  x_num <- as.numeric(x)
  
  # Apply labels
  labels <- c(income_labels, missing_labels)
  
  # Create labelled factor
  labelled_factor <- labelled(x_num, labels = labels)
  
  return(labelled_factor)
}

# Select and process income variables
cleaned_data <- merged_data %>%
  select(NSID, W8DINCB, W9DINCB) %>%
  mutate(
    inc25 = recode_income(W8DINCB, "inc25"),
    inc32 = recode_income(W9DINCB, "inc32")
  ) %>%
  select(NSID, inc25, inc32)

# Write output to CSV
write_csv(cleaned_data, "data/output/cleaned_data.csv")

# Print summary
cat("Cleaning complete!\n")
cat("Output file: data/output/cleaned_data.csv\n")
cat("Total records:", nrow(cleaned_data), "\n")
cat("Variables:", names(cleaned_data), "\n")
```
